name: Charlie Woerner
headline: Blocking Specialist, Not A Fantasy Asset
body:
Woerner's 2025 line tells the whole story: 8 catches on 9 targets for 48 yards across 7 games, a 1.7% target share that ranked him 84th among tight ends. His 50.8% snap rate looks respectable until you see it came mostly as a blocker, and a 35.9% route participation that produced just 0.24 yards per route run. The -0.022 EPA per play and 33.3% success rate confirm he moves the needle on the ground, not through the air.

Nothing about the profile points to a 2026 breakout. The 88.9% catch rate is empty efficiency on near-zero volume, with 1.33 air yards per target signaling a checkdown and short-area role. Atlanta values him as an in-line run blocker, and that ceiling caps any fantasy upside. Projections in the 14-reception, one-touchdown neighborhood say it plainly: leave him on the wire in every format.
